notes: First run of the CI runtime analyst — this file establishes the baseline. No prior week to compare against. Perceived-time percentiles are over the full population of successful runs. Execution time, runner wait, and segment medians are from a 6-run sample (one merge-group run per active day, spread across the week) because the jobs API is per-run and heavy; treat them as directional, not population medians.

webBuildStepMed.note: 07-01 value of 5s was a warm turbo-cache hit; excl. that, ~88-154s

pull_request.note: Recent sample only (30 most recent runs, 2026-07-07 15:31-19:00 window); not full-week population.

push.note: Recent sample only (30 most recent runs, 2026-07-06 15:00 - 2026-07-07 18:07 window). Push-to-main runs largely short-circuit because they reuse merge-group results, so p50 is dominated by skipped jobs.

flakyNote: No tests showed [retries=N] or [flaky] markers in the sampled run-tests logs (2026-07-07 tests-web client step + tests-worker step). Sample is limited; not a guarantee for the full week.

webClient.test: json-utils.clienttest.ts > Performance Comparison > Large Object Performance > 250K keys, depth 1 (~10MB)

workerServer.test: webhooks.test.ts > executeWebhook > reset failure count when webhook re-enabled

action.reason: First run establishes baseline; no confirmable week-over-week regression, no clean sandbox-verifiable win, no flaky tests to fix.
